commonAnnotations.summary: shipping rollout wedged: new revision has unavailable replicas past its progress deadline
commonAnnotations.description: The shipping deployment in {{NS}} started rolling revision 8 at 19:34, which set the container image to rlvr/shipping:v99-nonexistent. The new ReplicaSet shipping-7d9c4b6f88 cannot pull that image (ErrImagePull/ImagePullBackOff), so its pods {{POD}} and {{POD2}} are stuck Pending at 0/1 Ready. The Deployment Progressing condition flipped to False with reason ProgressDeadlineExceeded after the 30s progressDeadlineSeconds elapsed. max(kube_deployment_status_replicas_unavailable{deployment="shipping"}) = 2 against an SLO of 0. The old revision pod {{POD3}} (rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2) is still Running and serving, so capacity is reduced but not fully down.
commonAnnotations.error: StuckRollout: shipping new revision not progressing
commonAnnotations.suspected_symptom: deployment has unavailable replicas and a new revision that is not progressing past its deadline

adversarial_signals: The old-revision pod {{POD3}} (rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2) is Running, 1/1 Ready, restartCount 0 with memory_util_pct at 38% and CPU at 0.19 cores — well under its 512Mi / 500m limits, so this is not resource exhaustion or a runtime fault on the workload that is still serving.

The new-revision pods {{POD}} and {{POD2}} show restartCount 0 and have never run a container — this is NOT a CrashLoopBackOff; the containers are stuck in Waiting=ImagePullBackOff, so do not be misled into treating it as a crashing app.

{{NODE}} reported a kubelet heartbeat gap 16m ago but is Ready=True now and has ample allocatable memory; the new pods are unschedulable-to-run because of the image pull, not because the node is unhealthy or out of capacity.

A networkpolicy/shipping-egress was updated 22m ago, which looks like it could block the registry, but the kubelet event explicitly reports the registry returned 'manifest unknown' (the tag does not exist), not a timeout or connection refused — so egress/DNS to the registry is fine.
A HorizontalPodAutoscaler evaluated shipping during the window (desired 2, current 2, no change); replica count was never altered by autoscaling and the HPA is not implicated in the stuck rollout.

HPA evaluated shipping: desired 2, current 2 (no change) — red herring

annotations.summary: shipping deployment has 2 unavailable replicas; revision 8 (rlvr/shipping:v99-nonexistent) timed out past progressDeadlineSeconds 30s
shipping new ReplicaSet pods stuck in ImagePullBackOff — manifest unknown

ruling_out_keywords: new pods restartCount 0 ImagePullBackOff not a crashloop
old-revision pod {{POD3}} Running 1/1 Ready serving normally
memory 38% under 512Mi limit not resource exhaustion
cpu 0.19 cores under 500m limit not throttling
all three nodes Ready=True with capacity not node-local
registry returned manifest unknown not connection refused so egress/dns ok
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The shipping deployment shipped a bad image revision that cannot be pulled, so its rollout is wedged. Revision 8 set the container image to rlvr/shipping:v99-nonexistent at 19:34:02, creating ReplicaSet shipping-7d9c4b6f88. That tag does not exist in the registry (manifest unknown), so the new pods {{POD}} and {{POD2}} are stuck Pending at 0/1 with container state Waiting=ImagePullBackOff/ErrImagePull and restartCount 0 (they never started). After the 30s progressDeadlineSeconds elapsed, the Deployment Progressing condition flipped to False with reason ProgressDeadlineExceeded and Available flipped to False (MinimumReplicasUnavailable, 1/3 available). max(kube_deployment_status_replicas_unavailable{deployment="shipping"}) = 2 against an SLO of 0. The old-revision pod {{POD3}} (rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2, revision 7) is still Running and serving, so capacity is reduced but not fully down. Rolling the deployment back (rollback) to the previous good revision (revision 7, rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2) discards the unpullable ReplicaSet and drives replicas unavailable (kube_deployment_status_replicas_unavailable) to 0.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: bad_deploy
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Deployment is at current_revision 8 with image rlvr/shipping:v99-nonexistent, previous_image rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2 (revision 7); updated 2, ready 1, available 1, unavailable 2 [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- Progressing condition is False with reason ProgressDeadlineExceeded (progressDeadlineSeconds 30s); Available is False (MinimumReplicasUnavailable) [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- New-revision pods {{POD}} and {{POD2}} are Pending 0/1 with state Waiting reason ImagePullBackOff/ErrImagePull 'manifest unknown', restartCount 0 [evidence: k8s_pods]
- Events show ScalingReplicaSet for shipping-7d9c4b6f88 (v99-nonexistent), repeated Failed 'manifest unknown', and ProgressDeadlineExceeded on the deployment [evidence: k8s_events]
- max(kube_deployment_status_replicas_unavailable{deployment="shipping"}) = 2 breaches the SLO of 0 [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not a crashloop: the new pods have restartCount 0 and are stuck in ImagePullBackOff (the container never ran), unlike a CrashLoopBackOff which shows repeated restarts and terminated lastState [evidence: k8s_pods]
- Not resource exhaustion: the serving old-revision pod {{POD3}} runs at memory_util_pct 38% and cpu 0.19 cores, well under its 512Mi/500m limits [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Not node failure: all three nodes are Ready=True with ample allocatable memory; both stuck pods fail the same image pull regardless of node [evidence: k8s_node_health]
- Not a network/registry-connectivity fault: the kubelet reports 'manifest unknown' (the tag does not exist), not a timeout or connection refused; the networkpolicy/shipping-egress change is a red herring [evidence: k8s_events]
- Not a dependency failure: the old-revision pod's logs show db and dns healthy; the issue is purely the unpullable new image [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- Revision 8 rolls the shipping image to rlvr/shipping:v99-nonexistent at 19:34, creating ReplicaSet shipping-7d9c4b6f88
- The kubelet cannot pull that tag (manifest unknown); the new pods {{POD}}/{{POD2}} stay Pending in ImagePullBackOff and never become Ready
- After 30s the progress deadline is exceeded; Progressing flips to False (ProgressDeadlineExceeded) and Available flips to False (MinimumReplicasUnavailable)
- kube_deployment_status_replicas_unavailable climbs to 2, breaching the SLO of 0; StuckRollout/DeploymentReplicasUnavailable alerts fire
- The old-revision pod {{POD3}} keeps serving at reduced capacity (1/3)
- Rolling the deployment back to revision 7 (rlvr/shipping:v1.7.2) removes the unpullable ReplicaSet and drives replicas_unavailable back to 0
